title: John C. Dvorak has died
excerpt: Reports coming in of the death of John C. Dvorak, apparently passed away on Monday (July 20th) from complications of heart bypass surgery . ...
content_html: Reports coming in of the death of John C. Dvorak, apparently passed away on Monday (July 20th) from complications of heart bypass surgery. Dvorak started in wine and moved to silicon as an early columnist at InfoWorld, one of the pillar references for this particular blog because of its studious regular reporting, and most notably for PC Magazine where he had no less than two columns since at least 1986. In addition to this and other tech journalism credits, he was on the flagship CNET Central cable TV program in the mid 1990's with his "Buy It, Try It, Skip It" reviews, plus other shows hosted on NPR and then-ZDTV. He was also a regular guest on Leo LaPorte's syndicated radio program and podcasts, and joined former MTV veejay Adam Curry (well known to us in Gopherspace for MTV Networks v. Curry, and an early user of TTYtter before Twitter started to suck) in 2007 as co-host of the No Agenda podcast, a role he maintained until his death. Dvorak hailed from a different generation of tech journalists, far more personal and yet far more technical, and even his detractors (given his political views, which we won't talk about here, there are many) would admit he was at least entertaining. He was 80. Rest in peace.
content_text: Reports coming in of the death of John C. Dvorak, apparently passed away on Monday (July 20th) from complications of heart bypass surgery. Dvorak started in wine and moved to silicon as an early columnist at InfoWorld, one of the pillar references for this particular blog because of its studious regular reporting, and most notably for PC Magazine where he had no less than two columns since at least 1986. In addition to this and other tech journalism credits, he was on the flagship CNET Central cable TV program in the mid 1990's with his "Buy It, Try It, Skip It" reviews, plus other shows hosted on NPR and then-ZDTV. He was also a regular guest on Leo LaPorte's syndicated radio program and podcasts, and joined former MTV veejay Adam Curry (well known to us in Gopherspace for MTV Networks v. Curry, and an early user of TTYtter before Twitter started to suck) in 2007 as co-host of the No Agenda podcast, a role he maintained until his death. Dvorak hailed from a different generation of tech journalists, far more personal and yet far more technical, and even his detractors (given his political views, which we won't talk about here, there are many) would admit he was at least entertaining. He was 80. Rest in peace.

blocks.html_en: Reports coming in of the death of John C. Dvorak, apparently passed away on Monday (July 20th) from complications of heart bypass surgery. Dvorak started in wine and moved to silicon as an early columnist at InfoWorld, one of the pillar references for this particular blog because of its studious regular reporting, and most notably for PC Magazine where he had no less than two columns since at least 1986. In addition to this and other tech journalism credits, he was on the flagship CNET Central cable TV program in the mid 1990's with his "Buy It, Try It, Skip It" reviews, plus other shows hosted on NPR and then-ZDTV. He was also a regular guest on Leo LaPorte's syndicated radio program and podcasts, and joined former MTV veejay Adam Curry (well known to us in Gopherspace for MTV Networks v. Curry, and an early user of TTYtter before Twitter started to suck) in 2007 as co-host of the No Agenda podcast, a role he maintained until his death. Dvorak hailed from a different generation of tech journalists, far more personal and yet far more technical, and even his detractors (given his political views, which we won't talk about here, there are many) would admit he was at least entertaining. He was 80. Rest in peace.

sentences.en: Reports coming in of the death of John C.
sentences.zh: 关于约翰·C·约翰 (John C.) 去世的报道不断传来。

sentences.en: Dvorak, apparently passed away on Monday (July 20th) from complications of heart bypass surgery.
sentences.zh: 德沃夏克显然于周一（7 月 20 日）因心脏搭桥手术并发症去世。

sentences.en: Dvorak started in wine and moved to silicon as an early columnist at InfoWorld, one of the pillar references for this particular blog because of its studious regular reporting, and most notably for PC Magazine where he had no less than two columns since at least 1986.
sentences.zh: 德沃夏克最初从事葡萄酒行业，后来作为 InfoWorld 的早期专栏作家转向硅领域，该博客因其勤奋的定期报道而成为该特定博客的支柱参考之一，最引人注目的是 PC 杂志，至少自 1986 年以来，他在该杂志上至少有两篇专栏。

sentences.en: In addition to this and other tech journalism credits, he was on the flagship CNET Central cable TV program in the mid 1990's with his "Buy It, Try It, Skip It" reviews, plus other shows hosted on NPR and then-ZDTV.
sentences.zh: 除了这篇文章和其他科技新闻报道之外，他还在 1990 年代中期的旗舰 CNET Central 有线电视节目中发表了“买它、尝试它、跳过它”的评论，以及在 NPR 和当时的 ZDTV 上主持的其他节目。

sentences.en: He was also a regular guest on Leo LaPorte's syndicated radio program and podcasts, and joined former MTV veejay Adam Curry (well known to us in Gopherspace for MTV Networks v.
sentences.zh: 他还是 Leo LaPorte 的联合广播节目和播客的常客，并加入了前 MTV 主持人 Adam Curry（我们在 Gopherspace 中因 MTV Networks v.1 案而为我们所熟知）。

sentences.en: Curry, and an early user of TTYtter before Twitter started to suck) in 2007 as co-host of the No Agenda podcast, a role he maintained until his death.
sentences.zh: 库里（Curry，也是 Twitter 开始糟糕之前的 TTYtter 的早期用户）于 2007 年担任 No Agenda 播客的联合主持人，他一直担任这一职务直至去世。

sentences.en: Dvorak hailed from a different generation of tech journalists, far more personal and yet far more technical, and even his detractors (given his political views, which we won't talk about here, there are many) would admit he was at least entertaining.
sentences.zh: 德沃夏克来自不同一代的科技记者，他们更加个人化，但也更加技术化，甚至他的批评者（考虑到他的政治观点，我们不会在这里谈论，有很多）也会承认他至少很有趣。

content_html_zh: 关于约翰·C·约翰 (John C.) 去世的报道不断传来。 德沃夏克显然于周一（7 月 20 日）因心脏搭桥手术并发症去世。 德沃夏克最初从事葡萄酒行业，后来作为 InfoWorld 的早期专栏作家转向硅领域，该博客因其勤奋的定期报道而成为该特定博客的支柱参考之一，最引人注目的是 PC 杂志，至少自 1986 年以来，他在该杂志上至少有两篇专栏。 除了这篇文章和其他科技新闻报道之外，他还在 1990 年代中期的旗舰 CNET Central 有线电视节目中发表了“买它、尝试它、跳过它”的评论，以及在 NPR 和当时的 ZDTV 上主持的其他节目。 他还是 Leo LaPorte 的联合广播节目和播客的常客，并加入了前 MTV 主持人 Adam Curry（我们在 Gopherspace 中因 MTV Networks v.1 案而为我们所熟知）。 库里（Curry，也是 Twitter 开始糟糕之前的 TTYtter 的早期用户）于 2007 年担任 No Agenda 播客的联合主持人，他一直担任这一职务直至去世。 德沃夏克来自不同一代的科技记者，他们更加个人化，但也更加技术化，甚至他的批评者（考虑到他的政治观点，我们不会在这里谈论，有很多）也会承认他至少很有趣。 他80岁了。 安息。
